name: Orion
description: Enterprise-grade digital platform providing shared identity, security, session, and audit capabilities for medical-device-adjacent applications. Orion is invisible to clinicians and serves as the common substrate upon which multiple Medtronic products are built. It does not implement clinical logic or device interaction.
purpose: Orion exists to solve the structural problem of fragmented, duplicated infrastructure across medical device products. It centralizes identity, security, session management, and audit so products do not re-solve these safety-critical concerns independently.
what_breaks_without: Each product would re-implement identity, security, and session management independently. Inconsistencies would increase compliance risk. Cloud adoption would fragment. Architectural reasoning would become product-specific rather than system-level.

relationships.context: Provides session, identity, authorization, and audit services consumed by SmartSync Astra
Provides governance, identity, and audit infrastructure for remote monitoring

_comment: TYPE: component — Named functional unit within a product
name: Edge Safety Enforcement Module
description: Local guardrail component that enforces immediate safety constraints before actions reach the implanted device. Operates independently of cloud availability to ensure safety checks never depend on network connectivity.
responsibilities: Ensuring cloud unavailability does not compromise safety
why_exists: Safety checks must not depend on network availability. Cloud latency is unacceptable for immediate safety enforcement.

relationships.context: Validates commands before they reach the device mediator

name: Authorization and Policy Enforcement
description: Platform domain that decides what an authenticated actor is allowed to do. Centralizes policy definition, evaluation, and least-privilege enforcement. Products enforce outcomes locally but the platform decides outcomes. Does not own identity issuance or workflow logic.
responsibility_class: Determine what an authenticated actor is allowed to do under specific conditions
enforcement_model: Products submit action context to the platform policy engine, receive allow/deny decisions, and enforce them locally. Products must never define authorization rules or infer permission from past actions.

relationships.context: Authorization requires verified identity as input

_comment: TYPE: workflow — Named end-to-end behavioral sequence
name: Controlled Device Programming Workflow
description: End-to-end workflow for safely applying clinician-intended device configuration changes in SmartSync Astra. Separates intent capture from execution and enforces multiple safety gates before any device modification occurs.
trigger: Clinician initiates a device configuration change
steps: Validate proposed changes against safety constraints
Perform pre-command safety checks at edge
completion_condition: Device state verified after acknowledged programming command and audit record persisted
safety_gates: Loss of connectivity mid-command halts safely
No session means no device interaction
failure_handling: Partial execution triggers abort and state verification
Any ambiguity resolved to conservative outcome
invariants: No device interaction outside a session

relationships.context: Commands are routed through the device mediator
Pre-command safety checks enforced at the edge

_comment: TYPE: decision — Named architectural choice with trade-offs
name: Hybrid Edge-Cloud Architecture
description: Architectural decision to adopt a hybrid architecture with safety-critical execution at the edge and governance/orchestration in the cloud, rather than a pure cloud or pure local model.
decision_statement: Adopt a hybrid architecture with safety-critical execution at the edge and governance/orchestration in the cloud
rationale: Device interaction requires deterministic low-latency execution
Cloud connectivity cannot be assumed in clinical settings
Safety checks must not depend on network availability
Governance, audit, and consistency benefit from centralization
tradeoffs_accepted: Need for reconciliation logic between edge and cloud

alternatives_rejected.rejection_reason: Unsafe under latency or connectivity loss in clinical environments
Poor scalability, weak governance, inconsistent cross-product behavior

relationships.context: All safety-critical steps must execute at the edge, cloud is for governance only
Hybrid approach directly resolves the cloud vs safety tension

_comment: TYPE: constraint — Named boundary, non-goal, or limitation
name: CareLink Network Does Not Program or Control Devices
description: CareLink Network is strictly observational, not interventional. It never modifies device state, executes commands, or interacts with device firmware. This boundary is absolute and safety-critical.
why_it_exists: Remote monitoring lacks clinician presence and real-time safety guarantees. Device control without these safeguards would be unsafe and violate regulatory constraints.
contrast_with: SmartSync Astra executes controlled device programming within session-bounded, safety-gated workflows with clinician present.

relationships.context: Absolute boundary preventing any device modification capability

_comment: TYPE: data_concept — Named data entity, metric, or lifecycle state
name: Clinical Session
description: A bounded in-clinic interaction episode that serves as the primary correlation key across all SmartSync Astra product data. All meaningful actions, devices, and clinicians are bound within a session context. No meaningful data exists outside a session.
represents: A bounded, traceable, in-clinic interaction between a clinician and an implanted cardiac device
data_constraints: No meaningful data exists outside a session context
Session context cannot be transferred between actors
Concurrent sessions are isolated from each other

relationships.context: Session definition and invariants come from Orion platform semantics

_comment: TYPE: problem — Named problem domain that a product/platform solves
name: Fragmentation of Device-Centric Digital Systems
description: Historically, device-centric software systems evolved independently across product lines, each optimizing locally. This resulted in multiple incompatible approaches to device communication, identity, trust, and data handling across the ecosystem.
why_hard: Each product team optimized independently for local needs
Device communication approaches diverged across product lines
Identity, trust, and session assumptions were inconsistent
Integration complexity between products grew non-linearly
No single team had authority to unify approaches
consequences_without: Multiple incompatible approaches to device communication
Divergent assumptions about identity, trust, and session state
Inconsistent data representations for similar concepts
Reduced ability to reason about the ecosystem as a whole
how_solved: Provides shared abstractions for common device-adjacent concerns
Encourages consistent patterns across products via platform enforcement
Acts as a unifying layer that aligns architectural assumptions

relationships.context: Orion platform was created specifically to address ecosystem fragmentation
